description_long: This is a traditional pitchfork featuring a long, straight wooden handle and a set of four robust metal tines. The handle is cylindrical, smooth, and light brown, likely made from varnished hardwood, providing both durability and comfort for gripping. The tines are evenly spaced, straight, and made of dark metal, designed for efficient penetration and lifting of materials such as hay, compost, or debris. The transition between the handle and the tines is reinforced with a metal collar for added strength. There are no visible ergonomic grips or additional coverings at the end of the handle, giving it a classic utilitarian appearance. The overall construction suggests it is intended for manual labor in gardens, parks, or urban green spaces.
description: A classic pitchfork with a long wooden handle and four straight metal tines, designed for manual lifting and turning of materials. The handle is smooth and uncoated, while the tines are dark and robust.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the wooden handle and all four evenly spaced metal tines.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the handle and the alignment of the tines, with only one tine clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the handle and the backs of the four tines, similar to the front but with less detail on the tines' curvature.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the handle and a single visible tine.
